description_long: This is a vintage-style briefcase with a hard, rectangular shell covered in black material and a hinged lid lined with blue fabric on the interior. The inside features a fitted compartment with a control panel labeled 'Arcta', dials, switches, and two attached cables leading to handheld devices, suggesting it is a specialized equipment case. The lid has an oval mirror or window inset, and the case is secured with metal hardware at the corners and a brown leather handle at the front. The exterior is plain, with no visible external pockets or branding except for the script on the control panel. The overall construction appears robust, with the interior designed to securely house and organize the included devices.
description: A vintage black hard-shell briefcase with a blue-lined interior, fitted with a control panel and handheld devices, and a brown leather handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open lid with blue lining, oval mirror, control panel labeled 'Arcta', and two attached handheld devices.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the case, the open lid, and the arrangement of the interior compartments and cables.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain black exterior and the back edge of the open lid, with minimal visible features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the open lid, blue interior, and the positioning of the handheld devices and cables.
attribute_handle_type: Single fixed, arched handle made of brown leather, attached to the front with metal brackets.
attribute_lock_mechanism: Two small metal latches on the front edge of the case, likely snap or clasp type, securing the lid.
attribute_brand_logo: The word 'Arcta' is prominently displayed on the interior control panel, but no external brand logo is visible.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
